key_themes_this_week: Strait of Hormuz closure: 20% global oil supply + 25% global LNG disrupted; Brent at $145/bbl
Iran political risk at 3800 bps (highest in tracked universe, tied with Russia); Supreme Leader dead
Gulf state exposure: ARE, KWT, QAT, SAU, OMN all face IRGC strikes and Hormuz shutdown
Lebanon re-enters conflict: Hezbollah ceasefire collapse, Israeli incursion into south Lebanon
Pakistan-Afghanistan open war compounds South Asian instability
Dual chokepoint shock: energy importers (JPN, KOR, DEU, IND, CHN) face severe supply disruption
US political risk: first non-zero PRP (50 bps) in platform history; Section 122 tariff + domestic unrest
China military purge: largest since 2015; civil-military stability questions during Hormuz crisis
KOSPI -7.24% circuit breaker triggered in South Korea on oil shock exposure

events.headline: Iran: Week 2 of Operation Epic Fury — conflict ongoing; Hormuz Day 3; political risk at 3800 bps
events.details: US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury continues into week 2. Strikes targeting remaining IRGC command infrastructure. Strait of Hormuz closure now in its 3rd day — 21mbpd oil and 150bcm LNG blocked. Iran Interim Leadership Council governing (Pezeshkian, Mohseni-Eje'i, Arafi). Political risk premium raised to 3800 bps — tied with Russia as highest globally. Supply chain chokepoint exposure set to maximum (1.0). Economy on collapse trajectory.

events.headline: SYSTEMIC: Strait of Hormuz closed — 20% global oil supply + 25% global LNG disrupted; Brent at $145
events.details: Iran closed Strait of Hormuz on Mar 1. 21mbpd oil and 150bcm/yr LNG blocked. Affected: SAU, UAE, KWT, IRQ, QAT, OMN. Brent crude at $145/bbl (+35% from pre-conflict). Cape of Good Hope rerouting adds 2-3 weeks. Energy importers (JPN, KOR, DEU, IND, CHN) face severe shock. Duration unknown; IRGC maintains capability to sustain closure.

events.headline: Pakistan declares open war on Afghanistan; airstrikes on Kabul; domestic military deployment in 3 cities
events.details: Pakistan Air Force struck Kabul and eastern Afghan provinces targeting TTP. Army deployed to Karachi, Lahore, FATA border with cross-border pursuit ROE. 20 killed in domestic protests (PTI supporters); curfew in Karachi. PRP raised to 1925 bps. Nuclear arsenal security secondary concern.

events.headline: Lebanon: Hezbollah breaks ceasefire; Israel enters south Lebanon; Beirut airport intermittent closure
events.details: Hezbollah broke Nov 2024 ceasefire Mar 1, firing 200+ rockets at Haifa/northern Israel. IDF crossed Blue Line in Metulla/Shtula sectors. Israel struck 150+ Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon/Bekaa Valley. UNIFIL sheltering in place. Beirut airport intermittent closure. Lebanon economy faces fresh shock.

events.headline: Saudi Arabia: IRGC drone strikes US Embassy Riyadh; Aramco exports blocked; emergency OPEC+ meeting called
events.details: IRGC drones struck US Embassy Riyadh (no Saudi casualties). Ras Tanura and Ju'aymah export terminals blocked by Hormuz closure. PETROLINE (5mbpd) diverting some exports. Emergency OPEC+ meeting called. PRP raised from 305 to 405 bps; SCCE raised to 0.92.

events.headline: UAE: Al Minhad struck; Jebel Ali (world's 9th largest port) cut off; 7,700+ flight delays
events.details: IRGC ballistic missiles struck Al Minhad Air Base. Jebel Ali port cut off by Hormuz closure ($14bn/month trade affected). 7,700+ flight delays at DXB and AUH. UAE closed Tehran embassy. ADNOC exports near zero. PRP raised to 385 bps; SCCE raised to 0.97.

events.headline: Kuwait: Ali al-Salem Air Base struck; friendly fire incident; $2.5bn/month oil revenue loss
events.details: IRGC struck Ali al-Salem Air Base (hosts USAF 386th AEW). Friendly fire incident near Kuwait-Iraq border. Ahmadi port blocked by Hormuz closure. KPC estimating $2.5bn/month revenue loss. Kuwait exploring mediation role with Iran. PRP raised to 395 bps; SCCE raised to 0.95.

events.headline: South Korea: KOSPI -7.24% crash; circuit breakers triggered; BOK emergency measures under review
events.details: KOSPI fell 7.24%, triggering Level 2 circuit breaker. Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -8%. KRW fell 3.2% vs USD. BOK emergency cabinet meeting. Korea oil import bill up ~$85bn/year at current prices. PRP raised from 10 to 60 bps; SCCE raised from 0.593 to 0.82.

events.headline: US: Section 122 tariff (15%, 150-day clock) + growing domestic unrest from Iran war; first non-zero PRP
events.details: Trump invoked Section 122, 15% tariff effective Mar 3 (expires Jul 31 without Congressional extension). Protests in 23 cities; 847 arrests; National Guard activated 4 states. Inflation expectations: UMich 5yr at 4.1% (highest since 1995). First time USA has non-zero political risk premium (50 bps) in platform history.

events.headline: China: 19 senior PLA officers purged; largest military purge since 2015; CSI 300 -3.1%
events.details: 19 PLA senior officers removed including 3 theater commanders since Feb 15. Largest purge since 2015-2016. CMC vice chairs reshuffled. CSI 300 -3.1% on announcement. Taiwan Strait command structure uncertain. China faces Hormuz oil shock simultaneously. PRP raised from 80 to 180 bps; SCCE raised from 0.38 to 0.62.

events.headline: Qatar/Bahrain/Oman: Hormuz closure exposure at critical levels; Qatari LNG (20% global supply) blocked
events.details: Qatar LNG (77mtpa = 20% global supply) 100% blocked. Al Udeid struck. Bahrain 5th Fleet HQ at risk. Oman at Hormuz mouth — potential mediation role. All three countries SCCE at 0.95. Watch for escalation.

events.headline: Egypt: Suez Canal effectively closed; ~$800M/month canal revenue loss; FX reserves deteriorating
events.details: Houthi attacks reduced Suez Canal traffic by 70%+. Egypt losing ~$800M-$1bn/month in fees. Annual canal revenue to fall from $10.3bn to under $3bn. FX reserves under pressure. EGY PRP raised to 1050 bps; SCCE raised to 0.85.

events.headline: Germany/Japan face stagflation risk from dual Hormuz+Suez oil shock; SPR drawdown under discussion
events.details: Germany SCCE raised from 0.257 to 0.45; Japan SCCE raised from 0.542 to 0.78. Germany additional oil import cost ~$45bn/year; Japan ~$60bn/year. ECB and BOJ monitoring; G7 emergency energy meeting called; SPR drawdown being discussed.

events.headline: Sudan: SAF retakes Khartoum; 24.6M face acute hunger; Egypt absorbs 500,000+ refugees
events.details: SAF retook central Khartoum from RSF Feb 28-Mar 1 — largest SAF territorial gain since Apr 2023. 24.6M Sudanese face acute food insecurity (UN: world's largest humanitarian crisis). Egypt absorbing 500,000+ refugees with border crossings at capacity. RSF retreating to Darfur; war not over. Humanitarian corridors under negotiation.

events.headline: Myanmar: Military junta controls 21% of territory; resistance forces dominate north/west; economy collapsed
events.details: SAC controls 21% of territory (down from 33% mid-2024). 3BHA controls Shan/Sagaing/Chin. GDP -5.8%, inflation 32%, kyat at historic low. 18M+ needing food assistance. PRP at 2000 bps. Comprehensive sanctions. ASEAN process failed. Protracted civil war scenario.

events.headline: Ukraine: ~120 km² gained in Zaporizhzhia in Feb 2026; war in year 5 with no ceasefire in sight
events.details: Ukraine recaptured ~120 km² near Robotyne in Zaporizhzhia. Increased drone strikes on Crimea/Rostov logistics. EU €20bn additional support package (Feb 20). Russia responding with intensified Kharkiv/Kherson glide bomb campaign. CC/Ca credit rating; 2,750 bps PRP. Economy entirely dependent on Western financing.
